Morita Holdings Corporation ( (JP:6455) ) has shared an announcement.

Morita Holdings Corporation has completed the acquisition of 1,861,000 treasury shares at a total price of ¥4,658,083,000 through the Tokyo Stock Exchange’s Off-Auction Own Share Repurchase Transactions (ToSTNeT-3). This move is part of a flexible capital policy aimed at adapting to changes in the business environment and returning value to shareholders, as resolved by the Board of Directors.

Brambles ( (AU:BXB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

data.title: Pelthos Therapeutics Earnings Call Highlights Growth and Challenges
data.description:
Pelthos Therapeutics Inc. ((PTHS)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Pelthos Therapeutics Inc. recently held its earnings call, revealing a generally positive sentiment despite some financial challenges. The company celebrated the successful launch of ZELSUVMI and outlined strategic growth plans, including expanding its sales force and acquiring Xepi. While high operating losses and uncertain long-term GTN levels pose challenges, the overall outlook remains optimistic due to strong revenue growth and strategic positioning.

Successful Launch of ZELSUVMI

Pelthos Therapeutics marked a significant milestone with the launch of ZELSUVMI, the first FDA-approved at-home treatment for molluscum contagiosum. The product achieved strong initial demand, generating $7.1 million in net product revenue in Q3 2025. This success underscores Pelthos’ ability to innovate and meet market needs effectively.

Expansion of Sales Force

In response to the robust performance metrics of ZELSUVMI, Pelthos plans to expand its sales force by adding 14 new representatives. This strategic move aims to cover more metropolitan areas, thereby enhancing the company’s reach and accelerating growth.

Positive Revenue and Growth Metrics

Pelthos reported a remarkable 41% increase in prescribed units from September to October, achieving a $52 million annual gross revenue run rate by the last week of October. These metrics highlight the company’s strong market presence and potential for continued growth.

Strong Cash Position

The company concluded Q3 2025 with a solid cash position of $14.2 million and no debt, bolstered by an $18 million convertible notes financing. This financial stability provides a foundation for future investments and strategic initiatives.

Strategic Acquisition of Xepi

Pelthos’ acquisition of Xepi, an FDA-approved treatment for impetigo, complements its existing product portfolio. This strategic acquisition leverages Pelthos’ commercial infrastructure, enhancing its market offering and competitive edge.

Operating and Net Losses

Despite the positive developments, Pelthos reported an operating loss of $15.4 million and a GAAP net loss of $16.2 million for Q3 2025. These figures reflect the financial challenges the company faces as it invests in growth and expansion.

High Cost of Goods Sold

The cost of goods sold amounted to $2.3 million, influenced by the fair value step-up of inventory following the merger. This factor contributes to the company’s financial challenges in the short term.

Uncertain Long-term GTN Levels

While GTN rates were favorable at 25.3% in Q3, they are expected to rise to the mid- to high 30% range by Q1 2026. This uncertainty poses a challenge for Pelthos as it navigates future pricing strategies.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Pelthos provided forward-looking guidance during the earnings call, highlighting a strong initial demand for ZELSUVMI and expectations to reach cash flow breakeven by the end of 2026. The company plans to continue leveraging its $18 million convertible notes financing to support operations and strategic expansions, including the acquisition of Xepi. With a projected $52 million annual gross revenue run rate, Pelthos anticipates significant revenue growth in the coming quarters.

In summary, Pelthos Therapeutics Inc.’s earnings call reflected a positive sentiment, driven by the successful launch of ZELSUVMI and strategic growth initiatives. While financial challenges such as high operating losses and uncertain GTN levels persist, the company’s strong revenue growth and strategic positioning offer a promising outlook for the future.

data.title: Journey Medical’s Earnings Call Highlights Growth and Challenges
data.description:
Journey Medical Corporation ((DERM)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Journey Medical Corporation’s recent earnings call exuded a positive sentiment, largely fueled by impressive revenue growth and the successful launch of Amrozi. Despite facing challenges such as declining revenues from legacy products and reliance on co-pay assistance programs due to generic competition, the company is actively addressing these issues through strategic initiatives.

Strong Revenue Growth

Journey Medical Corporation reported a robust 21% year-over-year revenue growth in the third quarter of 2025, with total revenues reaching $17.6 million compared to $14.9 million in 2024. This growth underscores the company’s successful strategies in expanding its market presence and product offerings.

Successful Launch of Amrozi

Amrozi has significantly bolstered Journey Medical’s top line, contributing $4.9 million in Q3, which marks a 75% increase from Q2. The product’s prescription growth soared by 146%, with 18,198 prescriptions in Q3 compared to 7,394 in Q2, highlighting its strong market acceptance.

Positive Market Reception and Physician Feedback

The market reception for Amrozi has been overwhelmingly positive, with physicians praising its clinical benefits, including the onset of efficacy in as little as two weeks. Strong initial refill rates further indicate high satisfaction levels among prescribers and patients alike.

Improved Financial Metrics

Journey Medical has made strides in improving its financial metrics, with EBITDA improving by $500,000 from a loss of $1 million in 2024 to a loss of $500,000 in Q3 2025. The company also achieved a positive adjusted EBITDA of $1.7 million, compared to $300,000 in the previous year.

Impact of Generic Competition

The company faces challenges from generic competition, particularly affecting legacy products like Accutane, which saw a 16% decline in revenue year-over-year. This highlights the need for continued innovation and strategic positioning in the market.

Gross Margin Decrease

Journey Medical experienced a decrease in gross margin to 67.4% in Q3 2025 from 69.4% in the same period last year. This decline is attributed to favorable non-operational adjustments and product mix changes in Q3 2024.

Reliance on Co-pay Assistance Program

The company continues to depend on a co-pay assistance program as it navigates the slow adoption of health plan formularies, which impacts short-term financial performance. This reliance underscores the ongoing challenges in achieving widespread market access.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Journey Medical Corporation remains optimistic about its future, anticipating sustainable EBITDA positivity by Q4 2025. The company ended the quarter with $24.9 million in cash, up from $20.3 million at the end of 2024. Amrozi’s market access strategy has successfully secured over 100 million commercial lives, with plans to contract with a third major GPO early next year to expand coverage further.

In conclusion, Journey Medical Corporation’s earnings call highlighted a positive outlook, driven by strong revenue growth and the successful launch of Amrozi. Despite challenges from generic competition and reliance on co-pay assistance programs, the company is taking strategic steps to address these issues. Investors and market watchers can look forward to the company’s continued growth and financial improvements in the coming quarters.

data.title: Phoenix New Media’s Earnings Call Highlights Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Phoenix New Media ((FENG)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

The recent earnings call for Phoenix New Media painted a picture of strong performance with notable revenue growth and increased user engagement. The company reported significant improvements in its paid services and a reduction in net loss, although challenges remain with rising operating expenses and a difficult advertising market.

Significant Revenue Growth

Phoenix New Media experienced a remarkable 22.3% increase in total revenues year-on-year, reaching RMB 200.9 million from RMB 164.3 million. This substantial growth underscores the company’s ability to expand its financial footprint despite market challenges.

Paid Services Revenue Surge

The company saw an impressive 161.6% year-on-year surge in paid services revenues, totaling RMB 41.6 million. This growth was primarily driven by the success of its digital reading services, highlighting a lucrative area of expansion for Phoenix New Media.

Successful Media Influence and Events

Phoenix New Media’s influence was further demonstrated through its successful events, such as the Shanxi Culture and Tourism Promotion Event, which generated over 2 billion online impressions. This showcases the company’s strong media presence and ability to engage large audiences.

Decrease in Net Loss

In a positive turn, the net loss attributable to iFeng was reduced to RMB 4.9 million from RMB 18.5 million year-on-year. This reduction indicates improved financial health and effective cost management strategies by the company.

Strong User Engagement and Platform Growth

Phoenix New Media reported robust user engagement, with its video accounts gaining nearly 0.5 million new followers. Additionally, the company’s presence on RedNote expanded, reflecting its growing influence and reach in the digital space.

Increased Operating Expenses

Despite the positive financial indicators, the company faced a 23.6% rise in total operating expenses year-on-year, amounting to RMB 109 million. This increase was largely attributed to higher sales and marketing expenses, posing a challenge to maintaining profitability.

Challenging Ad Market Conditions

The advertising market presented difficulties for Phoenix New Media, with cautious client budgets and rapidly changing marketing dynamics hindering growth in advertising revenue. This remains a critical area for the company to address moving forward.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead to the fourth quarter, Phoenix New Media forecasts total revenues to range between RMB 205.9 million and RMB 220.9 million. Net advertising revenues are expected to be between RMB 171.4 million and RMB 181.4 million, while paid service revenues are anticipated to range from RMB 34.5 million to RMB 39.5 million. These projections indicate a continued focus on revenue growth despite the challenges faced.

In summary, Phoenix New Media’s earnings call highlighted a strong performance in revenue and user engagement, with significant strides in paid services and a reduction in net loss. However, the company must navigate rising operating expenses and a challenging advertising market to sustain its growth trajectory.

data.title: Tonies SE Celebrates Strong Q3 Earnings and Growth
data.description:
tonies SE Class A ((DE:TNIE)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

The recent earnings call for Tonies SE Class A was marked by a predominantly positive sentiment, with the company celebrating the successful launch of Toniebox 2, significant revenue growth, and robust global expansion. While there were minor criticisms about product features, these did not overshadow the overall optimistic outlook for the company.

Successful Launch of Toniebox 2

The launch of Toniebox 2 was a major highlight, representing the first significant innovation since 2016. This new product has opened up growth opportunities with new audiences and use cases. It has been well received, boasting a 4.6 average rating and high recommendation rates, indicating strong consumer satisfaction.

Record Revenue Growth

Tonies SE reported a remarkable revenue increase of over 52% in Q3, with year-to-date growth reaching 33%, totaling EUR 322 million. This growth was driven by all markets, with North America showing a 36% increase and the Rest of World segment surging by 80%.

Global Expansion and Brand Recognition

The company has expanded its reach to over 100 countries, achieving significant global media coverage with more than 1 billion impressions from the Toniebox 2 launch. The product’s success was further validated by winning the CES 2026 Best of Innovation Award.

Strong Strategic Partnerships

Tonies SE has strengthened its strategic partnerships, notably expanding its collaboration with Hasbro. This partnership will introduce iconic board games in new audio-first Tonieplay formats starting Q2 2026, highlighting the company’s commitment to growth through strategic alliances.

Growing International Revenue Share

The company’s international revenue share increased by 6 percentage points year-over-year to 59%, underscoring the success of its international expansion strategy.

Cable Length Criticism

Despite the overall positive reception, some customers criticized the short cable length of the Toniebox 2. This issue arose from regulatory requirements for 1+ certification, rather than cost-saving measures.

Temporary Shifts in Sales Patterns

The anticipation of the Toniebox 2 launch led to temporary shifts in sales patterns earlier in the year, affecting the share of Tonieboxes sold. However, this was expected and managed by the company.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Tonies SE confirmed its full-year guidance, projecting over 25% growth in group revenue to exceed EUR 600 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin between 6.5% and 8.5%. The company emphasized the strategic importance of the Toniebox 2 launch, noting its strong consumer response and resilience in the face of macroeconomic challenges, supported by sourcing flexibility and a strong pricing strategy.

In summary, the earnings call for Tonies SE Class A painted a positive picture of the company’s current performance and future prospects. With the successful launch of Toniebox 2, record revenue growth, and strategic global expansion, the company is well-positioned for continued success. Despite minor criticisms, the overall sentiment remains optimistic, with strong forward-looking guidance reinforcing confidence in the company’s trajectory.

data.title: Canadian Solar’s Earnings Call: Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Canadian Solar ((CSIQ)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Canadian Solar’s latest earnings call presented a balanced sentiment, highlighting both impressive growth and notable challenges. The company showcased strong advancements in energy storage and high-margin project sales, alongside strategic progress in US manufacturing. However, concerns were raised due to decreased solar segment margins, net losses impacted by shareholder actions, ongoing litigation, and increased debt levels.

Record Energy Storage Shipments

Canadian Solar achieved a record quarterly shipment of 2.7 gigawatt hours in energy storage, underscoring robust growth in this segment. This milestone reflects the company’s successful expansion and strategic focus on energy storage solutions.

Strong Revenue Performance

The company reported total revenue of $1.5 billion, reaching the high end of expectations. This strong revenue performance is indicative of Canadian Solar’s effective market strategies and operational execution.

Gross Margin Exceeded Expectations

Canadian Solar’s gross margin stood at 17.2%, surpassing guidance. This was primarily driven by significant contributions from energy storage shipments, highlighting the profitability of this segment.

Significant Progress in US Manufacturing

The company is making notable strides in US manufacturing, with phase one of the solar cell factory in Indiana and the lithium battery factory in Kentucky on track to start production by the end of 2026. These developments are expected to strengthen the US supply chain.

Growth in Residential Energy Storage

Canadian Solar is experiencing strong growth in residential energy storage markets in Japan, Italy, and the US, while expanding into Germany and Australia. The company is on track to achieve profitability in this segment by 2025.

High-Margin Project Sales

The company monetized over 500 megawatts of projects, achieving a gross margin of 46.1%. This was driven by profitable project sales, demonstrating Canadian Solar’s ability to capitalize on high-margin opportunities.

Decreased Gross Margin in Solar Segment

The solar segment experienced a gross margin decrease of 730 basis points to 15%, attributed to incremental upstream price increases and underutilization. This highlights a key area of concern for the company.

Net Loss Due to Preferred Shareholder Impact

Canadian Solar reported a net income attributable to shareholders of $9 million, translating to a net loss of $0.07 per diluted share. This was primarily due to the impact of paid-in-kind preferred shareholder actions.

Challenges with ADCVD Process

The company is facing ongoing litigation related to the ADCVD process, which could result in significant financial liabilities. Despite this, no reserve has been booked, indicating uncertainty in this area.

Reduced Cash Flow and Increased Debt

Canadian Solar reported net cash used in operating activities of $1.112 billion, with total debt rising incrementally to $6.4 billion. This increase in debt is a critical area of concern for the company’s financial health.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Canadian Solar expects fourth-quarter revenue between $1.3 and $1.5 billion, with a gross margin of 14 to 16%. For 2026, the company projects module shipments ranging from 25 to 30 gigawatts, including one gigawatt for internal projects, and energy storage shipments between 14 and 17 gigawatt hours. The company is also focusing on strategic US manufacturing investments and compliance with the One Big Beautiful Bill Act to support long-term growth and market positioning.

In conclusion, Canadian Solar’s earnings call highlighted a mix of strong growth and strategic progress, alongside challenges that need addressing. The company’s advancements in energy storage and US manufacturing are promising, while concerns about solar segment margins, shareholder impacts, and increased debt remain areas to watch closely.

data.title: Deutsche Telekom’s Earnings Call Highlights Growth and Strategic Initiatives
data.description:
Deutsche Telekom ((DTEGY)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Deutsche Telekom’s recent earnings call exuded a generally positive sentiment, underscored by notable achievements in growth metrics, strategic initiatives, and shareholder returns. Despite encountering some challenges in the German market, the company demonstrated resilience and strategic foresight.

Consistent Growth Metrics

Deutsche Telekom showcased robust performance with a 3.7% growth in organic service revenue, 4.4% increase in organic EBITDA, 6.8% rise in free cash flow, and a 9.5% growth in adjusted earnings per share for the first nine months of 2025. These metrics underscore the company’s strong financial health and operational efficiency.

T-Mobile US Performance

T-Mobile US delivered impressive Q3 results, reporting a 9.1% growth in service revenue and a 5.6% increase in core EBITDA. The company exceeded expectations with 2.3 million postpaid net additions, highlighting its competitive edge in the U.S. market.

Dividend and Share Buyback

The Board of Management proposed an 11% increase in dividends to EUR 1 per share for 2025 and announced plans to buy back EUR 2 billion worth of shares in 2026. This initiative aims to return nearly EUR 7 billion to shareholders, reflecting the company’s commitment to enhancing shareholder value.

Fiber Expansion in Europe

Deutsche Telekom made significant strides in fiber expansion, passing 3.6 million additional European homes with FTTH over the past year, reaching nearly 23 million homes. This expansion is pivotal in strengthening its infrastructure and service offerings across Europe.

Moody’s Credit Rating Upgrade

Moody’s upgraded Deutsche Telekom’s credit rating to A3, acknowledging the company’s disciplined financial execution. This upgrade is a testament to Deutsche Telekom’s robust financial management and strategic initiatives.

AI Initiatives and Partnerships

In a significant move towards AI-driven digitization, Deutsche Telekom launched Europe’s first industrial AI cloud in partnership with NVIDIA, with a combined investment of EUR 1 billion. This initiative marks a substantial advancement in the company’s digital transformation efforts.

T-Systems Growth

T-Systems reported an impressive 11.7% year-to-date EBITDA growth, driven by AI-driven digital solutions and sovereign cloud services. This growth highlights the division’s pivotal role in Deutsche Telekom’s overall strategy.

Challenges in German Market

The German market faced a weaker quarter with a 1.8% decline in headline growth, attributed to challenging prior year comparisons and cost phasing, including wage increases. This underscores the competitive and economic pressures in the region.

Broadband Net Additions in Germany

Deutsche Telekom experienced a slight customer loss in Germany’s broadband market, influenced by competitive pressures and a sluggish German economy. This challenge highlights the need for strategic adjustments in the domestic market.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Deutsche Telekom raised its guidance to reflect T-Mobile’s improved performance, maintaining its full-year DT ex U.S. guidance. The company plans to increase its dividend by 11% to EUR 1 per share for 2025 and buy back EUR 2 billion worth of shares in 2026, aiming for a shareholder return of nearly EUR 7 billion. Strategic progress in Germany with record fiber build and AI initiatives across Europe were also highlighted.

In summary, Deutsche Telekom’s earnings call reflected a positive outlook with strong growth metrics and strategic initiatives, despite some challenges in the German market. The company’s commitment to shareholder returns and strategic investments in AI and fiber expansion are key takeaways, positioning it well for future growth.

data.title: Ascendis Pharma A/S: Strong Growth and Strategic Challenges
data.description:
Ascendis Pharma A/S ((ASND)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Ascendis Pharma A/S Reports Strong Revenue Growth Amidst Challenges

The recent earnings call for Ascendis Pharma A/S painted a picture of robust revenue growth and global market expansion, particularly for their products Eurvipath and Skytrofa. The company celebrated regulatory approvals and a positive operating profit, though it acknowledged ongoing challenges with insurance approval conversion rates and increased SG&A expenses due to commercial expansion. Additionally, foreign currency impacts posed a challenge to revenue growth.

Strong Global Launch of Eurvipath

Eurvipath has made a significant impact in the global market, achieving €143 million in revenue for Q3. In the US alone, over 4,250 patients have been prescribed Eurvipath by more than 2,000 healthcare providers. With insurance approval rates at approximately 70%, the product is now available in over 30 countries, marking a successful global launch.

Positive Operating Profit

Ascendis Pharma reported a positive operating profit of €11 million in Q3, a testament to their financial transformation. This positive result reflects sustained revenue and cash flow growth, indicating a promising financial trajectory for the company.

Regulatory Approvals and Progress

The company achieved significant regulatory milestones with FDA approval for TransCon Growth Hormone for adult growth hormone deficiency in the US and MAA authorization in the EU for pediatric growth hormone deficiency. Additionally, TransCon CNP is under priority review in the US, with a PDUFA date set for November 30.

Expansion of Commercial Activities

Skytrofa generated €51 million in revenue, supported by the initiation of a Phase 3 basket trial. TransCon CNP also showed promising trial results, demonstrating improvements in linear growth and other health metrics, further solidifying Ascendis Pharma’s commercial expansion.

Conversion Rate Challenges

Despite the success of Eurvipath, the insurance approval conversion rate remains at 70%. The company is focused on improving this rate over time and aims to reduce the time required for approval conversions.

Foreign Currency Impact

Revenue growth for Q3 was partially offset by foreign currency headwinds, with a €3.6 million impact on Eurvipath and a €1.6 million impact on Skytrofa, highlighting the challenges of operating in a global market.

SG&A Expense Increase

The company’s SG&A expenses rose significantly to €113.4 million in Q3 2025, up from €69.8 million the previous year. This increase reflects the costs associated with global commercial expansion, underscoring the company’s commitment to growth.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Ascendis Pharma anticipates sustained revenue growth driven by new patient enrollments, geographic expansion, and additional market entries for Eurvipath and Skytrofa. The company ended the quarter with €539 million in cash and cash equivalents, demonstrating strong cash flow management and positioning itself well for future growth.

In summary, Ascendis Pharma’s earnings call highlighted a period of strong financial performance and strategic growth. While challenges such as insurance approval conversion rates and foreign currency impacts remain, the company’s positive operating profit and regulatory advancements provide a solid foundation for continued success. Investors and stakeholders can look forward to Ascendis Pharma’s sustained growth and market expansion in the coming quarters.
